name: Savory Cabbage Strudel
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt butter in a large heavy saucepan.
2. Add cabbage and bok choy greens, currants (optional), onion, and scallions to the pan. Cook over medium-high heat for 3-4 minutes or until the greens begin to wilt.
3. Add dill, cilantro, and salt; cook for 1 minute over high heat, stirring well to thoroughly incorporate herbs into vegetables. Set aside.
4. In a separate large skillet, melt butter.
5. Add mushrooms and saute briefly over high heat, stirring frequently until they begin to soften. Remove from heat, sprinkle with lemon juice, and add to the vegetable mixture. Mix well.
6. Preheat oven to 400°F.
7. Strain mushroom-vegetable mixture in a colander, catching the liquid in a small bowl (reserve for soups, gravies, etc.).
8. Lightly butter a cookie sheet.
9. Place one phyllo sheet on damp towel, brushing lightly with melted butter.
10. Place second phyllo sheet on top of the first.
11. Sprinkle 2 tablespoons of bread crumbs on the left-hand side of the phyllo.
12. Fold the right side over to encase the bread crumbs, making a rectangle.
13. Brush the top lightly with melted butter.
14. Spread half the vegetable mixture along the long edge, stopping about 1 inch short of the sides.
15. Roll as you would a jelly roll.
16. Place the seam-side down on the buttered baking sheet. Brush the roll with melted butter.
17. Repeat for the second roll.
18. Bake until golden, about 30 minutes.
19. Cut each into 3-4 sections.
